title: Tounatea mollis
taxonomy: Tounatea mollis is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is native to tropical Africa. The classification of Tounatea mollis places it within the genus Tounatea, which is closely related to the genus Anodendon. This genus is further classified within the subfamily Rauvolfioideae. Tounatea mollis is often found growing in shaded areas of the forest.
uses: Tounatea mollis has been traditionally used in medicine, with its extracts showing potential antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. intro: Tounatea mollis, also known as Toona, is a plant species belonging to the mahogany family (Meliaceae). The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant activities. The bioactive constituents of Tounatea mollis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects.
